items.title: The signal bitcoin momentum traders have been waiting for is here
items.desc: What you need to know for April 22, 2026

items.title: Coinbase-incubated Base launches Azul testnet upgrade with multiproofs to advance decentralization
items.desc: Base rolled out its Azul upgrade on testnet, introducing multiproofs as the Coinbase-incubated Ethereum L2 eyes Stage 2 decentralization.

items.title: Coinbase shifts New York prediction markets fight to federal court
items.desc: Coinbase legal chief Paul Grewal says the company removed New York’s prediction markets lawsuit to federal court, setting up a sharper fight over CFTC authority and state gambling laws.

items.title: PENGU Notches Double-Digit Gains as Bitcoin Hits $78K Amid $418M Liquidation Spree
items.desc: The crypto market outlook improved as altcoins rallied amid Bitcoin's $78,000 retest, but experts remain cautious.

items.title: Bitcoin tests $78,000 resistance as short-squeeze risks mount, altcoins rally
items.desc: BTC hovers at key $78,000 level with $180 million liquidations at stake, while altcoins and memecoins surge as risk-on sentiment returns.

items.title: UK investors regain tax-free access to crypto ETNs via Innovative Finance ISA route
items.desc: Stratiphy secured IFISA approval, reopening a tax-free ISA route for UK retail investors to hold 21Shares crypto ETNs.

items.title: Bitcoin 'Bull Score' hits six-month high as 2022 bear-market fears linger
items.desc: Bitcoin price metrics saw a broad recovery in April, but analysis warns that the 2022 bear-market breakdown could still repeat.

items.title: Another DeFi protocol hacked as Sui-based Volo hit by $3.5M exploit
items.desc: Volo Protocol has confirmed a $3.5 million exploit affecting select vaults, adding that it has frozen assets and started fund recovery efforts amid ongoing investigation.

items.title: Flight to safety: How Maker’s Spark and USDC are winning the $10 billion Aave breakup
items.desc: Funds leaving Aave are splitting across safer lending, simpler ETH exposure and off-chain yield, with stablecoins acting as a temporary refuge.

items.title: Uzbekistan creates state-backed crypto mining zone with tax breaks
items.desc: Uzbekistan has created a supervised crypto mining zone in Karakalpakstan, allowing foreign sales while requiring proceeds to flow through local bank accounts.

items.title: Traders don’t see Kelp socializing losses after $292 million exploit
items.desc: Polymarket prices low odds of a system-wide redistribution, as the protocol weighs how to handle an undercollateralized rsETH supply

items.title: Bitcoin rises above $78,000, Fear & Greed Index exits ‘extreme fear’ zone
items.desc: Bitcoin climbed above $78,000 early Wednesday, with analysts citing short squeezes and strong ETF inflows as drivers of the move.

items.title: Stratiphy reopens tax-free route to crypto ETNs for UK investors
items.desc: Stratiphy is reopening a tax-free route to crypto ETNs in the UK after HMRC’s ISA rule change left retail investors with little practical access.

items.title: A make or break moment: why $79,200 could act as a launchpad or a ceiling for bitcoin
items.desc: True Market Mean and Short-Term Holder cost basis form a critical $78.2K to $79.2K range that could define the next major move.

items.title: Another DeFi protocol loses millions in hack days after KelpDAO breach
items.desc: Volo Protocol lost about $3.5 million from three vaults holding WBTC, XAUm, and USDC.

items.title: Russia passes crypto bill in first reading; permits use in foreign trade settlements
items.desc: The bill would prohibit the use of crypto for domestic payments but allow its use in foreign economic activities, according to TASS.

items.title: Bitcoin's 'Coinbase premium' just posted its longest bullish streak since October's record high of $126,000
items.desc: Bitcoin's Coinbase premium has been positive for 14 straight days, the longest bullish streak since BTC hit its all time high of $126,000 in October.

items.title: Privacy protocol Umbra shuts front end to stifle Kelp exploiters
items.desc: Umbra has shut down its front end to stop hackers, but says it can’t stop the use of its smart contracts or another version of its open-source front end.

items.title: Chinese crypto mogul Li Lin’s private trading arm is set to move into a Hong Kong-listed wealth firm
items.desc: The transfer effectively moves part of Li’s private trading operation into a public company where he is the largest shareholder.

items.title: Coinbase highlights Algorand, Aptos' work to mitigate quantum threat
items.desc: Coinbase’s quantum advisory board says quantum computing isn’t yet a threat, but has urged for upgrade work to begin, with some blockchains being less prepared than others.

items.title: Justin Sun sues World Liberty Financial over WLFI token freeze, governance exclusion
items.desc: Sun said the WLFI team refused to unfreeze his tokens, leaving him with no choice but to turn to the courts.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.

items.title: Inside the MAS Sandbox: How Ripple is testing RLUSD for real trade settlements
items.desc: How Ripple is testing RLUSD in Singapore’s MAS sandbox to enable conditional trade settlements, and why this does not amount to full regulatory approval.

items.title: Sui-based Volo Protocol exploited; team pledges to absorb losses
items.desc: Sui-based liquid staking platform Volo reported that it was exploited for roughly $3.5 million from its WBTC, XAUm, and USDC vaults.

items.title: Relics of a Revolution, Part IV: The Bank Was Already Burning
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Relics of a Revolution, Part IV: The Bank Was Already Burning A conversation with artist Alex Schaefer. This post Relics of a Revolution, Part IV: The Bank Was Already Burning first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Dennis Koch.

items.title: Kelp DAO exploiter launders $80 million worth of ETH, mostly through THORChain: onchain analyst
items.desc: The 24-hour swap volume on THORChain surged to $394 million, significantly exceeding usual daily volumes of under $35 million.

items.title: US admiral calls Bitcoin an instrument for US ‘power projection’
items.desc: US Navy Admiral Samuel Paparo said Bitcoin’s proof-of-work technology has "really important" computer science applications when it comes to cybersecurity.

items.title: Bitcoin Optech Newsletter #401 Recap Podcast
items.desc: Mark “Murch” Erhardt, Gustavo Flores Echaiz, and Mike Schmidt are joined by Remix7531 and Luis Schwab to discuss Newsletter #401.

items.title: Crypto's great hope in Senate's Clarity Act still has a path to survive tight calendar
items.desc: A sideshow stablecoin yield debate has dragged the market structure bill through months of delay, even as the Senate's available floor time diminishes for 2026.

items.title: Inside the hunt for Satoshi: Filmmakers chase crypto’s biggest mystery
items.desc: A new documentary digs into Bitcoin’s origins, reframing the search for Satoshi Nakamoto as both a technical investigation and a deeply human story.

items.title: Coinbase Flags Proof-of-Stake Chains Like Ethereum, Solana as Potential Quantum Risks
items.desc: Advisory council says validator signatures and wallet cryptography could be vulnerable if future quantum computers break current encryption.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.desc: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: New York sues Coinbase, Gemini over prediction market offerings
items.desc: New York has become the latest state to argue that prediction market contracts touching on sports and entertainment violate state gambling laws.

items.title: Etherealize updates long-term ETH price prediction to $250,000
items.desc: ETH would trade above $250,000 if Ethereum can capture the same monetary premium as bitcoin and gold carry as stores-of-value.

items.title: Strategy Now Holds $62 Billion in Bitcoin—These Are Its Biggest BTC Buys
items.desc: How did Michael Saylor's firm amass a record stash of Bitcoin? Here's a look back at how Strategy made such massive gains.

items.title: DoorDash to offer stablecoin payments to users via Tempo blockchain
items.desc: Tempo announced the integration of ”stablecoin-powered payment infrastructure” with DoorDash, Stripe, Paradigm, Coastal Bank and ARQ.

items.title: Prediction markets are the new secret weapon for Coinbase and Robinhood growth
items.desc: Cantor Fitzgerald analysts said the market is treating recent trading slumps as old news, shifting focus instead toward prediction markets and new product launches to drive the next leg of growth for Coinbase and Robinhood.

items.title: Blockchain.com adds perpetual futures trading to self-custody wallets
items.desc: With a CFTC greenlight expected soon, industry momentum is building for the derivative contracts still only available to non-US investors.

items.title: Fed pick Warsh backs crypto’s place in finance as Warren raises ‘sock puppet’ concerns in hearing
items.desc: Digital assets are already ingrained in the financial industry, said Federal Reserve chair pick Kevin Warsh during his nomination hearing.

items.title: Mob boss John Gotti’s grandson is headed to prison for a $1.1 million Covid fraud and crypto scheme
items.desc: he mob boss’ grandson defrauded the U.S. government’s Covid-19 relief system out of $1.1 million and invested at least half of it to invest in crypto businesses.

items.title: SoFi adds XRP deposits but users criticize lack of external wallet transfers
items.desc: According to SoFi's support page, the crypto platform does not permit XR transfers to outside wallets.

items.title: Peter Thiel-backed unicorn Ramp rolls out $0 conversions between USDT and dollars across product suite
items.desc: The fintech provider has rolled out support for Ethereum, Solana and Plasma-issued USDT tokens across its entire product suite.

items.title: Tether Asserts Stablecoin Dominance Over Circle's USDC Amid Major Crypto Hacks
items.desc: Tether's USDT market cap hit an all-time high, with DeFi users appearing to favor the stablecoin over USDC as a refuge from industry fallout.

items.title: Crypto's massive exploit may force big banks to rethink their blockchain plans, Jefferies warns
items.desc: The $293 million Kelp DAO exploit has exposed critical infrastructure risks, leading Jefferies to suggest that traditional financial firms may pause their blockchain initiatives to prioritize security.

items.title: Bitcoin inflows to Binance fall to 2023 low as BTC bulls set target on $80K
items.desc: Bitcoin inflows diverge across exchanges as selling pressure eases on Binance, while Coinbase shows more dominant activity.

items.title: New York targets Coinbase, Gemini in fresh crackdown on prediction markets
items.desc: Attorney General Letitia James alleged that Coinbase and Gemini ran unlicensed markets, adding pressure on crypto companies as states move to regulate event-based trading platforms.

items.title: Bitcoin slides toward $75,000 amid Warsh hearing, stalling U.S.-Iran talks
items.desc: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq gave back their early morning gains alongside BTC as markets digested the Fed Chair nominee's Senate confirmation hearing and report of stalled Iran talks.

items.title: New York Sues Coinbase and Gemini Over Prediction Markets, Seeking Billions in Damages
items.desc: "Gemini and Coinbase’s so-called prediction markets are just illegal gambling operations," alleged New York AG Letitia James.

items.title: New York Sues Coinbase and Gemini Over Alleged Illegal Prediction Market Gambling Operations
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine New York Sues Coinbase and Gemini Over Alleged Illegal Prediction Market Gambling Operations New York Attorney General Letitia James has sued Coinbase and Gemini, alleging their prediction market platforms constitute illegal gambling operations. This post New York Sues Coinbase and Gemini Over Alleged Illegal Prediction Market Gambling Operations first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: New York Attorney General sues Coinbase, Gemini for ‘illegal gambling’ on prediction market platforms
items.desc: NY AG Letitia James has sued Coinbase and Gemini for allegedly illegally offering people the ability to bet on events.

items.title: Dogecoin Foundation, MoonPay Team to Donate 1 Million DOGE to Dog Charity
items.desc: Crypto payments firm MoonPay enabled contributions to dog welfare programs, marking a practical use case for Dogecoin.

items.title: Nium taps Coinbase to add USDC into global payments network
items.desc: Nium integrates Coinbase’s infrastructure to enable USDC-based cross-border payments, allowing businesses to settle in fiat or stablecoins without relying on prefunded accounts.

items.title: New York Attorney General Sues Coinbase, Gemini Over Unlicensed Prediction Markets
items.desc: New York AG Letitia James filed suit against Coinbase and Gemini, alleging their prediction market platforms constitute illegal gambling operations lacking state licenses.

items.title: Aave Partially Unfreezes WETH After Kelp Bridge Exploit
items.desc: After attackers deposited rsETH from an exploited Kelp bridge and borrowed Wrapped ETH, Aave had frozen WETH across multiple markets.

items.title: Bitcoin price rally in progress but upside may be capped at $84K
items.desc: Analysts say Bitcoin’s rally has begun, but the upside may be capped at an average spot BTC ETF cost basis near $84,000.

items.title: Bitcoin funding stays negative at $78K as short squeeze expectations grow
items.desc: BTC price action slowly headed upward but funding stayed negative, a unique occurrence that analysis said would likely result in a short squeeze.

items.title: Fed chair nominee pressed on potential conflicts of interest, independence
items.desc: Kevin Warsh faced intense questions from Senator Elizabeth Warren and other lawmakers over his more than $100 million financial disclosure ahead of a confirmation hearing.

items.title: Bitcoin regains $76K as Coinbase-driven demand sustains recovery
items.desc: A $517 million rise in spot volume led by Coinbase is helping Bitcoin reclaim $76,000, with steady inflows absorbing the weekend selling pressure.

items.title: Bipartisan PACE Act looks to open Fed payment rails to nonbanks, draws crypto support
items.desc: California Reps. Sam Liccardo and Young Kim introduced a bill that would allow certain providers access to the central bank.

items.title: Bitcoin Whales Accumulate 45,000 BTC as Warsh and Paparo Back Bitcoin’s Role
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin Whales Accumulate 45,000 BTC as Warsh and Paparo Back Bitcoin’s Role Bitcoin rose to around $76,000 as whales accumulated 45,000 BTC and institutional inflows surged, while U.S. officials including Kevin Warsh and Admiral Paparo signaled growing acceptance of Bitcoin in finance and national security. This post Bitcoin Whales Accumulate 45,000 BTC as Warsh and Paparo Back Bitcoin’s Role first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: DoorDash Teams Up with Tempo on Stablecoin Payments for Its Global Marktplace
items.desc: Tempo also announced it's launching a Stablecoin Advisory.

items.title: DoorDash to Pay Delivery Workers in Stablecoins via Stripe's Tempo Blockchain
items.desc: Delivery firm DoorDash will use Stripe's payments-focused blockchain, Tempo, to pay its Dashers in stablecoins in more than 40 countries.

items.title: Aave’s core markets hit 100% utilization at once, and that's not a good thing
items.desc: CertiK says the lending protocol is in serious trouble while CEO Stani Kulechov tells CoinDesk he does “not have anything useful to say.”

items.title: Top U.S. Pacific Commander Calls Bitcoin a “Valuable Computer Science Tool” for National Power And Security
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Top U.S. Pacific Commander Calls Bitcoin a “Valuable Computer Science Tool” for National Power And Security Admiral Samuel Paparo of U.S. Indo-Pacific Command told the Senate Armed Services Committee that Bitcoin is a “valuable computer science tool as power projection." This post Top U.S. Pacific Commander Calls Bitcoin a “Valuable Computer Science Tool” for National Power And Security first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: ‘Are we an industry of clowns?’: DeFi grapples with security tradeoffs after $292M Kelp DAO fallout
items.desc: Aftermath conditions from Kelp DAO’s exploit has opened a broader debate over DeFi’s security model, after billions left Aave.

items.title: USDT Now Live on Solana, Plasma, and Ethereum With 1:1 USD Onramps and Offramps: Privy and Ramp
items.desc: Ramp expands stablecoin access by launching USDT across Solana, Plasma, and Ethereum with seamless 1:1 USD conversion for global money movement.

items.title: Coinbase advisory board says quantum computing threat is on the horizon, crypto needs a plan
items.desc: The 50-page paper concludes that while today’s blockchains remain secure, a future “fault-tolerant quantum computer” capable of breaking widely used encryption is increasingly plausible, and preparation must begin now.

items.title: Poland stalls on crypto law, forcing local companies to move abroad
items.desc: Poland’s parliament is in a deadlock over crypto and its inability to align local laws with MiCA may drive some local crypto companies abroad.

items.title: European investors may switch banks for better crypto access, survey finds
items.desc: A survey by Börse Stuttgart Digital shows that 35% of European investors might switch to banks with better crypto offerings, but regulatory uncertainty remains an issue.

items.title: Arbitrum Freezes 30,766 ETH Linked to KelpDAO Exploit
items.desc: Since the freeze, ZachXBT reported that the attackers had begun moving funds from Ethereum mainnet to Bitcoin.

items.title: Fake delivery driver targets French crypto worker in armed home invasion
items.desc: A thwarted home invasion near Montpellier adds to France’s surge in violent “wrench attacks,” as high‑profile and doxxed crypto holders are increasingly in criminals’ sights.

items.title: Privacy Boost, Sunnyside's Privacy SDK, Goes Live on Optimism Mainnet: Optimism
items.desc: Optimism's first privacy offering by core developer Sunnyside uses ZK and TEE hybrid technology to enable confidential computing for enterprises on the OP Stack.

items.title: Cut the red tape: 39 financial giants demand an emergency fast-track for Europe's blockchain pilot
items.desc: The firms requested separating the DLT pilot regime from a larger package of 18 financial laws to allow for quicker updates and build real markets.

items.title: Group of 39 firms urge EU to fast-track DLT rules, warn of lagging US
items.desc: A 39-member coalition including Nasdaq urged the EU to fast-track DLT pilot changes, warning Europe could lose ground to the US in tokenized finance.

items.title: Core Scientific plans $3.3 billion debt offering, with proceeds to help repay credit facility loans
items.desc: Core Scientific plans a $3.3 billion private notes offering, with proceeds partly earmarked to repay credit facility borrowings.

items.title: Morgan Stanley’s Spot Bitcoin ETF Tops $139M in Assets Within Nine Days
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Morgan Stanley’s Spot Bitcoin ETF Tops $139M in Assets Within Nine Days Morgan Stanley’s spot Bitcoin ETF has accumulated over $139 million in BTC within nine days of launch, indicating strong early institutional demand for the firm’s new crypto offering. This post Morgan Stanley’s Spot Bitcoin ETF Tops $139M in Assets Within Nine Days first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: UK plans payments rule changes for stablecoins, tokenized deposits
items.desc: The UK government plans a stablecoin and tokenization rules overhaul, appointing former FCA official Chris Woolard to support work on its digital markets strategy.

items.title: Block’s Cash App Launches Accounts for Young Kids—Without Bitcoin Access
items.desc: Cash App joins the youth banking race with high-yield savings, but the managed kid accounts can’t tap into the app’s Bitcoin features.

items.title: Optimism Bills 'Privacy Boost' as Turning Point for Enterprises on Ethereum
items.desc: The layer-2 Ethereum scaling network’s privacy offering is expected to expand to additional blockchains.

items.title: South Korea tax agency opens bidding for crypto tracing tool
items.desc: South Korea's tax authority is looking to build crypto transaction tracking software that can help track potential tax evaders.

items.title: Bank of Korea's New Governor Prioritizes CBDCs Over Stablecoins in First Policy Address
items.desc: The central bank chief outlined digital finance priorities while legislative debates continue over private stablecoin frameworks.

items.title: Onramp Launches Finance Platform Combining Cash, Bitcoin and Gold in One Account
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Onramp Launches Finance Platform Combining Cash, Bitcoin and Gold in One Account Onramp launched a unified platform today combining cash management, bitcoin services, and gold ownership into a single account aimed at long-term wealth building. This post Onramp Launches Finance Platform Combining Cash, Bitcoin and Gold in One Account first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Morning Minute: Saylor's $2.54B Buy Buoys Bitcoin
items.desc: Michael Saylor and Tom Lee continued to buy BTC and ETH in big size, while Aave published its incident report from the KelpDAO exploit.

items.title: Grandson of Mob Boss John Gotti Sentenced for Funneling Stolen COVID Funds Into Crypto
items.desc: The grandson of Gambino crime boss John Gotti pleaded guilty to wire fraud after funneling $420K in stolen pandemic relief funds into crypto.

items.title: Bitcoin Resistance at $78K and $83K Could Cap Rally: Schwab
items.desc: The Schwab Center for Financial Research warned that Bitcoin faces key resistance between $78,000 and $83,000 investor cost basis levels.

items.title: Arbitrum Security Council Freezes $71.5M in Ethereum Linked to $292M KelpDAO Exploit
items.desc: Arbitrum's emergency response to the KelpDAO exploit has sparked praise and criticism over the network's ability to freeze stolen assets.

items.title: DeFi Protocols Launch Joint Escape Hatch for Aave ETH Lenders and Loopers
items.desc: Fluid's aWETH Redemption Protocol, launched with Lido, Ether.fi, 1inch, 0x and Kyber, has processed $136M out of Aave's frozen WETH pool in 48 hours.

items.title: UK Gas Firm Faces Pushback Over Plans to Mine Bitcoin
items.desc: Reabold Resources faced criticism for plans to use a gas field to mine Bitcoin, but said serving U.K. energy demand remains its main focus.

items.title: Aave Models $124M to $230M in Bad Debt From Kelp Exploit
items.desc: In a detailed incident report, Aave service providers quantified the protocol's exposure for the first time and outlined two scenarios depending on how Kelp DAO allocates the loss. LayerZero and Kelp continue to blame each other for the compromised bridge configuration.

items.title: Bitcoin Price Retakes $76,500 as Iran Tensions and Oil Volatility Drive Market Uncertainty
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin Price Retakes $76,500 as Iran Tensions and Oil Volatility Drive Market Uncertainty Bitcoin price held above $76,500 after slipping toward $75,000 over the weekend as US–Iran tensions and oil concerns weighed on markets. This post Bitcoin Price Retakes $76,500 as Iran Tensions and Oil Volatility Drive Market Uncertainty first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Dune Analytics Reveals 47% of LayerZero OApps Use Minimal DVN Security Following KelpDAO Hack
items.desc: Analysis of 2,665 LayerZero OApp contracts shows nearly half rely on single-validator security configurations, the same setup that exposed KelpDAO's rsETH to exploitation.

items.title: Hardware Wallet Tangem Announces Global Rollout of Its Retail Payments Service
items.desc: Tangem Pay lets users spend USDC directly from their self-custodial Tangem wallets, settling all transactions on Polygon.

items.title: DeFi Contagion Spreads Beyond Aave as LayerZero, Lido, Ethena Suffer Sharp Declines: Santiment
items.desc: AAVE, ZRO, LDO, and ENA tokens plunged 10–22% as market repriced risk across DeFi protocols exposed to a bad debt event, with LayerZero priced as equally culpable as the originating lender.

items.title: LayerZero Post Mortem Shows Lazarus Group Stole $290M From KelpDAO via RPC Node Compromise
items.desc: North Korea's Lazarus Group exploited a single-verifier LayerZero setup to drain $290M in rsETH on April 18 by compromising RPC infrastructure and poisoning the bridge's data feeds.
